title: JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 Overview

detail.title: JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 Overview
detail.short_info: JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 is out. Check how to download your response sheet, direct link to candidate portal, key dates, answer key and result schedule.

content.text: JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 has been released, and candidates can now download their recorded responses for both papers from the official JEE (Advanced) 2026 candidate portal at jeeadv.ac.in.

IIT Roorkee has made the copy of candidate responses available online as per the official schedule, so students can log in and check how they have attempted each question before the provisional answer key and result. This response sheet is crucial for estimating your probable score and planning the next steps in the admission process.
Direct Link to Download JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 Here

links.text: Direct Link to Download JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 Here

content.text: The overview below will help candidates quickly understand the key information about the JEE Advanced 2026 response sheet release.

rows: Copy of candidate responses available online
Registration number, date of birth, and other portal details

sections.heading: JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 Link at jeeadv.ac.in

content.text: The JEE Advanced 2026 response sheet is available only through the official JEE (Advanced) website, jeeadv.ac.in, under the candidate portal section. Candidates must avoid unofficial links and access the response sheet using the official login interface to ensure accuracy and security of their data.

Once the link labelled “Copy of Candidate Responses” or “JEE Advanced 2026 Response Sheet” is activated on the homepage, students can click it and log in with their credentials to view their recorded answers for both Paper 1 and Paper 2.

sections.heading: How to Download JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026

content.text: Step-by-Step Process to View Responses
Candidates can follow these simple steps to download their JEE Advanced 2026 response sheet from the official portal.

items: Visit the official JEE (Advanced) website: jeeadv.ac.in.
On the homepage, click on the link for “JEE Advanced 2026 Response Sheet” or “Copy of Candidate Responses.”
You will be redirected to the candidate portal (for example, cportal.jeeadv.ac.in) where login is required.
Enter your JEE Advanced 2026 registration number, date of birth and other required details as mentioned on the screen.
Click on the “Submit” or “Login” button to access your account.
Under the candidate dashboard, locate the “View Responses” or “Response Sheet” section.
Click to open your response sheet for Paper 1 and Paper 2 and verify the recorded answers.
Download the response sheet in PDF format and save it for future reference and score calculation.

content.text: Details Required to Login
To download the JEE Advanced response sheet 2026, candidates should keep the following login details ready as per the official portal requirements.

items: JEE Advanced 2026 registration number
Date of birth (in the format specified on the portal)
Additional fields such as registered mobile number or password, if asked on the candidate portal

content.text: Entering accurate login credentials is essential, as incorrect entries may prevent access to the response sheet or show no data.

sections.heading: JEE Advanced 2026 Response Sheet Release Date and Schedule

content.text: The release of the response sheet is part of a broader JEE Advanced 2026 schedule that includes provisional answer keys, objection submission, and final results.
Response Sheet, Answer Key and Result Dates
According to the official schedule on the JEE (Advanced) 2026 website, the following key dates are important for candidates.

items: Copy of candidate responses available: May 21, 2026 at 17:00 IST (5 PM).
Provisional answer keys display: May 25, 2026 at 10:00 IST (10 AM).
Feedback and comments on provisional answer keys: From May 25, 2026 (10:00 AM) to May 26, 2026 (5:00 PM).
Online declaration of final answer key and JEE Advanced 2026 result: June 1, 2026 at 10:00 IST (10 AM).

content.text: Students should regularly check the official website for any updates or changes to this schedule and remember that the response sheet is usually released before the provisional answer key.

sections.heading: How to Use JEE Advanced Response Sheet 2026 to Estimate Score

content.text: The primary purpose of the JEE Advanced response sheet is to let candidates verify their marked responses and estimate their probable score before the results are out. Once the provisional answer key is released, candidates can compare each response with the official correct answer to calculate their marks.
Matching Responses with Provisional Answer Key
After downloading the response sheet, candidates should wait for the provisional answer key, which is scheduled to be published on May 25, 2026. When both documents are available, you can:

items: Place the response sheet and provisional answer key side by side.
For each question, check whether your recorded response matches the correct option in the answer key.
Note questions left unattempted and those where you might have chosen an incorrect option.

content.text: This process helps you understand your performance in each section and identify topics where you did well or need improvement.
Calculating Probable Marks
JEE Advanced uses its own marking scheme, including full, partial and negative marks depending on the question type. Using the response sheet and answer key:

items: Add marks for each correct response as per the marking scheme for that particular question.
Subtract marks for questions with negative marking if your response does not match the correct answer.
Sum the scores for all questions across Paper 1 and Paper 2 to get an approximate total.

content.text: This estimated score is only indicative and the final score will be based on the official evaluation and final answer key.

sections.heading: JEE Advanced 2026 Exam - Basic Details (For Reference)

content.text: JEE Advanced is conducted for admission to undergraduate programmes in engineering, sciences and architecture at the Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs). For 2026, IIT Roorkee is the organising institute and the exam consists of two compulsory papers conducted on the same day.

Each paper typically includes questions from Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics, with a mix of single-correct, multiple-correct and numerical answer types. Candidates must appear in both Paper 1 and Paper 2 to be considered for ranking, and the total score is calculated based on performance in both papers combined.

sections.heading: Important Instructions for JEE Advanced 2026 Candidates

content.text: Candidates should follow certain important instructions while checking their JEE Advanced 2026 response sheet and using it for evaluation.

items: Ensure you download response sheets for both Paper 1 and Paper 2 and verify that your name and roll number are correctly mentioned.
Always download the response sheet from the official website only and avoid sharing your login details with others.
Save the PDF on a secure device and consider taking a printout for easy comparison with the provisional answer key.
Use the response sheet only to estimate your score and not as final proof of marks, since the official result may consider other evaluation factors.
If you find discrepancies related to question options or answers, wait for the provisional answer key and use the official objection mechanism within the given window.

content.text: Following these instructions will help you make the best use of your response sheet without relying on unverified information from external sources.
